tasks.task: Download Auntu 14.04 LTS from www.abb.com/desktop and follow the instructions provided on the website.
tasks.description: Ensure you click download, scroll down to take me to the download option, or donate if preferred. The ISO file will be used for virtual machine installation.

tasks.task: Understand that Auntu is a Linux distribution and it comes in various flavors.
tasks.description: Explore Debian, Ubuntu, and others as examples of different distributions to better understand the concept. Note that you only need one distribution for all Linux-based operations.

tasks.task: Choose the appropriate version of Auntu based on personal needs (LTS or later versions for new features).
tasks.description: For desktop use, LTS versions are recommended as they provide a stable environment with support for ongoing bug fixes. Later versions may have newer features but come with less stability guarantees.

tasks.task: Prepare to install Auntu on a virtual machine using the provided ISO file.
tasks.description: Understand that since the video is about installing Linux in a virtual environment, you'll be working directly with the raw ISO file rather than burning it to DVD.

tasks.task: Wait for download completion and ensure the ISO file is ready for installation.
tasks.description: Once downloaded, prepare to use the ISO file as the source for installing Auntu in a virtual machine environment.
